questions: What CLI-side spec deviations remain after prior implementation runs?
Should the CLI --rating flag be optional for reply posts?
Does the SKILL.md need updates for the icon change from heart to double thumbs up?

answers.answer: One confirmed deviation: --rating is required for all posts including replies via requireFlag (comments-post.ts:29). SKILL.md and all other CLI files are complete and correct.
evidence: comments-post.ts:29 — requireFlag(args, '--rating', '...') makes rating mandatory for all posts
Research report: 'When parentCommentId is present, rating becomes optional'
All 7 new files and 2 modified files structurally complete per scout

answers.answer: Yes. The spec says rating is optional for replies. When --reply-to is present, --rating should use getFlag instead of requireFlag so it's optional. When --reply-to is absent (top-level post), --rating remains required.
evidence: Server library-comment-service.ts:69-73 — validates rating required only for top-level
Research report Zod schema: 'When parentCommentId is present, rating becomes optional'

answers.answer: SKILL.md uses 'love' as the CLI flag value which maps to 'LOVE' stored value. The flag name stays 'love' regardless of icon changes (icon is UI-only). SKILL.md description can optionally be updated for clarity but no functional change is needed. The rating taxonomy description at SKILL.md:170 is correct.

statement: The CLI needs 1 targeted fix: make --rating optional when --reply-to is present in comments-post.ts. Change requireFlag to conditional getFlag based on whether --reply-to is set. SKILL.md is correct and complete. No new files needed.
